rules: Expanded to 16 teams — bottom 14 plus 4 play-in losers (both 9/10 and 7/8 losers from each conference)
Relegation zone (seeds 1–3): 2 balls each — 5.4% pick-1 odds. Being the worst is actively punished
Mid-lottery zone (seeds 4–10): 3 balls each — 8.1% pick-1 odds. Highest chance at the top pick
9/10 play-in losers: 2 balls each (5.4%). 7/8 play-in losers: 1 ball each (2.7%)
Floor picks: seeds 1–3 cannot fall below pick 12, seeds 4–10 cannot fall below pick 16
Consecutive win restriction: no team can hold the #1 pick in back-to-back years
Sunset clause: system reviewed and voted on after the 2029 draft
callout: Under this system, seeds 4-10 (8.1%) have better pick-1 odds than seeds 1-3 (5.4%). Being the worst team is punished — tanking for the top pick becomes a losing strategy.
